slides.title: 1-4. YANG Data Model for BGP Outbound Route Filtering
1-5-2. Fully Adaptive Routing Ethernet in Multi-Plane Scale-Out Networks (FARE in MPSON)
1-7. BGP best path next-hop selection enhancements
1-9. Canonical textual representation of BGP Path Attributes
1-11. Threat Model for BGP Color Extended Community

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: Apropos adding more sessions, meetings aren't highways but the famous highway paradox appears to apply to idr: https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/why-widening-highways-doesnt-fix-traffic-but-congestion-pricing-can/

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: Does this mean we need congestion pricing? Welllllllll...

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: We will do the orf model after Xiaohu's presentatinos.  There is a discrepancy in chair slide numbering and the agenda. Our apologies.

chat.author: Jie Dong
chat.text: Welcome to join the collaborative note taking: https://notes.ietf.org/notes-ietf-126-lsvr

chat.author: Fan Zhang
chat.text: No worries, thanks

chat.author: Ketan Talaulikar
chat.text: I request the authors of this ORF yang model to review and respond on the WGLC of the base BGP model that they propose to augment :folded_hands:

chat.author: Jie Dong
chat.text: Sorry, the link for IDR note taking is at: https://notes.ietf.org/notes-ietf-126-idr?both

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: For the RR draft, some overlapping GROW work: draft-ietf-grow-routing-ops-sec-inform

chat.author: James Cumming
chat.text: For the RR draft, consider how/if the RRs being on generalised compute impacts the recommendations.  Additionally, consider how ORR might play a role in design decisions.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text:
For John, we also have in YANG a "feature" as a concept. Aligning them is just picking some names and discussing how to make sure elements are flagged hard as optional is what's needed.  
We'll try something and see how it works out

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: https://github.com/ietf-wg-idr/RFC4271bis/issues - please consider filing there.

chat.author: Susan Hares
chat.text: Sunday LACNIC - indicate that Origin is used in South America for operational.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: Item for study for how origin field is being used is whether it's intended to have inter-domain impact, or solely impacting intra-domain and the use case is leaky.

chat.author: Susan Hares
chat.text: Would you review all potential Color sources (CAR/CT/RFC9012), Extended Community)

chat.author: Susan Hares
chat.text:
draft-vroonen-idr-bgp-bestpath-nh-selection-03

draft I reference.

chat.author: Jie Dong
chat.text: CPR RFC9723 may also needs to be taken into consideration?

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: For anyone remote, the speaker is being clear but projecting softly.  Sorry if hard to hear.

chat.author: Ian Dickinson
chat.text: impossible

chat.author: Ian Dickinson
chat.text: to understand

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: completely inaudible volume-wise?

chat.author: Robert Raszuk
chat.text: would help to angle the mic

chat.author: Ian Dickinson
chat.text: extremely low mumbling even with max volume

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: mic was dead

chat.author: Antonio De Abreu Faria
chat.text: Much better.

chat.author: Ian Dickinson
chat.text: much better thanks

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Was the mic turned off, or out of batteries? I can notify the AV team

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: dead batteries Please notify them. I've sent mail to support

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Ack

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: I'm struggling to understand what this brings to the table that flow-spec doesn't. I'd say it at the mic if we weren't out of time.

chat.author: Nat Kao
chat.text: Same. Why not use FS to do this?

chat.author: Ketan Talaulikar
chat.text: Thanks Kamiel for bringing your problem statement and solution for discussion and feedback to IDR as a new participant and an operator.

chat.author: Keyur Patel
chat.text: he seems to be talking about a preemptive mechanism.. versus FS which is a reactive means

chat.author: Keyur Patel
chat.text: thanks Kamiel!!

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: ISTM either FS or this thing can be done preemptively or (more realistically) reactively.

chat.author: Susan Hares
chat.text: @Kamiel - thanks for coming, see above comment on color.

chat.author: Keyur Patel
chat.text: @jgs - You would need an augmentation to RPKI that creates the source address list.. and then I think your suggesting to insert that in FS?

chat.author: Susan Hares
chat.text: @keyur + John - inserting in FSv2 would be an interesting use.

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: @Keyur Patel I guess? Maybe part of the intended value of the proposal is that it uses the RPKI to allow customers to publish their requested mitigations in a standardized way rather than their provider's private portal. If so, yes, the RPKI is a publication mechanism, the objects don't have to be stuffed directly into the protocol, necessarily. We already do this for OV, for instance.

chat.author: Keyur Patel
chat.text: @jgs - correct

chat.author: Robert Raszuk
chat.text: we spoke about using FS on the list. Kamiel says it is too inflexible and hard to deploy in the internet as it requires new AFI/SAFI ...

chat.author: Robert Raszuk
chat.text: and he has a point ... to me the most scary part is the uncontrolled blast radius of his new attribute

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: I don't see how it's harder to deploy than this whole new technology but I'm probably missing some detail.

chat.author: Robert Raszuk
chat.text: well adding an attribute to BGP is clearly easier .. if no one will react it is just a waist but sender part is done :)

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: I guess it's the interdomain piece that's new, which is also what scares you (me, too). I am an interdomain SAV skeptic... if we reduce it to a single AS case I think the FS comparison is more apt.

chat.author: Keyur Patel
chat.text: @Robert - if RPKI is used to advertise (as the proposal states) then the attribute announcement could be control (so would a FS entry). Dont see a difference there

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: The longer conversation about FS, even with validation hardening, is you don't want this as internet-wide FS.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: This is a strongly coupled dest route + source filter.  It's that strong coupling that makes this challenging in a normal unicast destination routed internet.

chat.author: Susan Hares
chat.text: @Maria - +1 on good work.  Ext-Comm is mess.  Would like to discuss FlowSpec v2 Extended communities.  See wiki at https://wiki.ietf.org/group/idr/FSv2-Alloc/actions/FSv2-extcom

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: The source-specific things also plays havoc with SAV tables providers would have to build.

chat.author: Robert Raszuk
chat.text: It seems that the notion of src-dst routing is coming at us from various angles ..

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: we reinvent it in some form every 3 years or so.

chat.author: Keyur Patel
chat.text: hmm.. I dont get this extension.. isn't it an implementation specific change?

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: As a piece of historical trivia, if I remember right either RFC 1771 or some other piece of BGP history specified that a route must not be advertised back to the AS it came from, but this was later removed for reasons. (Maybe related to some idea about multidomain multicast routing that never came to fruition.)

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: it's just a tweak vs. 5004.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: it was the msdp-v12 (iirc) thing, john.

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: But you can easily enough not do it, it's a super simple filter. I wouldn't be shocked if it were already a best practice.

chat.author: Keyur Patel
chat.text: @jgs - update groups

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: "to enable interdomain multicast".  msdp eventually changed it.

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: Oh yeah, MSDP.

chat.author: krishnaswamy ananthamurthy
chat.text: Amny implmentations have old best  path to prefer in case of conflict. This not new

chat.author: Susan Hares
chat.text: @John Scudder - IT was also an earlier RFC1771 thing Yakov had another plan for.  MDSP was the 2nd.

chat.author: Susan Hares
chat.text: I think it may be covered in bgp-issues document.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: the desire, krishnaswamy, is to have more consistent selection. I agree you can do stuff at this step transparently and not perturb the domain

chat.author: John Scudder
chat.text: Is anyone actually promiscuously forwarding based on Color received from the big I Internet? If "yes" then ZOMG.
